perturbation.original_text: In connection with Schneider serving on the Company's board, Schneider and other Company employees, directors, and agents may divulge nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries to the Stilwell Group and such information may be shared among the Stilwell Group's employees and agents who have a need to know such information.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with Schneider serving on the Business's board, Schneider and other Company employees, directors, and agents may divulge nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries to the Stilwell Group and such information may be shared among the Stilwell Group's employees and agents who have a need to know such information.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company's board' is replaced with 'Business's board'. The document consistently uses the term 'Company' elsewhere to refer to First Financial Northwest, Inc. The change introduces uncertainty whether 'Business' refers to the same entity. This in-text contradiction impacts all sections referring to the 'Company', making it unclear to which entity these sections apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with Schneider serving on the Business's board, Schneider and other Company employees, directors, and agents may divulge nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries to the Stilwell Group and such information may be shared among the Stilwell Group's employees and agents who have a need to know such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company's board' with 'Business's board'. The explanation states that the document consistently uses 'Company' to refer to First Financial Northwest, Inc., and the change introduces uncertainty whether 'Business' refers to the same entity. The contradicted text also uses 'Business's board'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the reader cannot be certain if 'Business' and 'Company' refer to the same entity, leading to ambiguity in the obligations and rights outlined in the contract, specifically regarding the sharing of nonpublic information. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, as it introduces conflicting obligations depending on which entity is referenced.

perturbation.original_text: The Stilwell Group expressly agrees to maintain all nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stilwell Group expressly agrees to maintain all private information concerning the Subsidiary and its subsidiaries in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'nonpublic information concerning the Company' is replaced with 'private information concerning the Subsidiary'. The text consistently uses the term 'nonpublic information' and 'Company' throughout the document. This perturbation introduces ambiguity as it is not clear whether 'private information' and 'nonpublic information' are the same. Also, the substitution of 'Company' with 'Subsidiary' can create internal contradiction since in this statement, Subsidiary is said to have its own subsidiaries. The location where it is contradicting is within Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Stilwell Group expressly agrees to maintain all private information concerning the Subsidiary and its subsidiaries in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text substitutes 'nonpublic information concerning the Company' with 'private information concerning the Subsidiary'. According to the provided explanation, this introduces ambiguity, as it's unclear if 'private information' and 'nonpublic information' are the same. Furthermore, replacing 'Company' with 'Subsidiary' creates a contradiction because the Subsidiary is now stated to have its own subsidiaries, which conflicts with the understanding within Section 1, where the contradicted text is located. This misalignment in terminology, where terms are used interchangeably without definition, leads to conflicting obligations, as outlined in the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Company's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Organization's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company's insider trading and disclosure policies' is replaced with 'Organization's insider trading and disclosure policies'. The document consistently uses 'Company' to refer to First Financial Northwest, Inc. The use of the term 'Organization' here creates ambiguity. It is not clear whether the term 'Organization' refers to the same entity as the 'Company'. This is an in-text contradiction due to the introduction of 'Organization' and its implications for the responsibilities of the Stilwell Group, especially when the rest of the sentence still references the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Organization's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company's insider trading and disclosure policies' with 'Organization's insider trading and disclosure policies'. The contradicted text also uses 'Organization's insider trading and disclosure policies' in the same location. According to the explanation, the document consistently uses 'Company' to refer to First Financial Northwest, Inc., and the use of 'Organization' introduces ambiguity because it's not clear if 'Organization' refers to the same entity as the 'Company'. This misalignment in terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction, affecting the obligations of the Stilwell Group.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent the nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries received by the Stilwell Group is material, this Agreement is intended to satisfy the confidentiality agreement exclusion of Regulation FD of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") set forth in Section 243.100(b)(2)(ii) of Regulation FD.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent the confidential data concerning the Business and its affiliates received by the Stilwell Group is material, this Agreement is intended to satisfy the confidentiality agreement exclusion of Regulation FD of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") set forth in Section 243.100(b)(2)(ii) of Regulation FD.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries' is replaced with 'confidential data concerning the Business and its affiliates'. The document consistently uses 'nonpublic information' and 'Company'. This creates uncertainty as it is not explicitly defined whether 'confidential data' is equivalent to 'nonpublic information,' or if 'Business' is equivalent to 'Company,' or if 'affiliates' is equivalent to 'subsidiaries'. This change introduces confusion to Section 1, as it is unclear to which entity's confidential data the agreement refers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To the extent the confidential data concerning the Business and its affiliates received by the Stilwell Group is material, this Agreement is intended to satisfy the confidentiality agreement exclusion of Regulation FD of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") set forth in Section 243.100(b)(2)(ii) of Regulation FD.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Company and its subsidiaries' with 'Business and its affiliates.' The explanation states this creates uncertainty as it's unclear if 'confidential data' equates to 'nonpublic information,' or if 'Business' equates to 'Company,' or if 'affiliates' equates to 'subsidiaries.' This change, according to the explanation, introduces confusion in the section. Since the modified and contradicted texts are the same and rely on undefined terms, this aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Company that: (a) he satisfies all of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws and any additional applicable qualifications under the laws of the State of Washington or under the regulations of any bank regulatory authority, and that he is not in any way precluded from serving as a director by order or other action of any court, -1- regulatory or other governmental authority; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, under Item 401(f) of SEC Regulation S-K.
perturbation.changed_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Firm that: (a) he satisfies all of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws and any additional applicable qualifications under the laws of the State of Washington or under the regulations of any bank regulatory authority, and that he is not in any way precluded from serving as a director by order or other action of any court, -1- regulatory or other governmental authority; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, under Item 401(f) of SEC Regulation S-K.
perturbation.explanation: Here the 'Company' is replaced with the term 'Firm'. By using the term 'Firm', in Section 3 the document creates an in-text contradiction. This is becaus Schneider now represents and warrants to the Firm that he satisfies qualifications to be a director *of the Company*. Because the terms have been changed, it is no longer clear that they are the same entity, and so his qualification to be director of the Company does not apply to his representations to the Firm. This ambiguity undermines the purpose of the warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Firm that: (a) he satisfies all of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws and any additional applicable qualifications under the laws of the State of Washington or under the regulations of any bank regulatory authority, and that he is not in any way precluded from serving as a director by order or other action of any court, -1- regulatory or other governmental authority; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, under Item 401(f) of SEC Regulation S-K.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Company', whereas the changed text refers to the 'Firm'. The contradicted text refers to the 'Firm' in place of 'Company', however the qualification refers to 'Company'. This creates a contradiction because Schneider is warranting that he is qualified to be a director of the Company, while representing and warranting to the Firm. Because the terms have been changed and are misaligned, it is no longer clear that they are the same entity, and so his qualification to be director of the Company does not necessarily apply to his representations to the Firm, resulting in a clear contradiction.
